headline: Micron (MU) Stock Gets Fair Value Boost On AI Contract And Margin Optimism
summary: Micron Technology’s updated fair value estimate has moved from US$866.60 to US$1,457.68, sharpening the focus on how analysts are rethinking what the stock might be worth. That higher target range sits against a backdrop of bullish commentary around multi year revenue contracts, high modeled gross margins, and AI driven demand, while more cautious voices point to rich expectations and the possibility that the current memory cycle eventually cools. Read on to see how these competing views are...

Read on to see how these competing views are...

headline: The Bull Case For Micron Technology (MU) Could Change Following GM Long-Term Auto Memory Supply Deal
summary: In late June 2026, Micron Technology signed a long-term Strategic Customer Agreement with General Motors to supply LPDRAM, NOR and UFS NAND memory and storage for current and next-generation vehicles, underpinned by Micron’s US$2.00 billion Manassas, Virginia DRAM fab modernization that began production earlier this year. This deal adds automotive to Micron’s growing base of multi-year supply contracts, illustrating how AI-era memory shortages are pushing major OEMs to lock in dedicated...
